name: Bhutan
avoid: Note: Sustainable Development Fee of ~$100/day applies

cities.name: Thimphu
cities.notes: Tashichho Dzong, Memorial Chorten and weekend vegetable market

cities.name: Paro
cities.notes: Tiger's Nest Monastery cliff hike, Rinpung Dzong and apple orchards

cities.name: Punakha
cities.notes: Punakha Dzong at the confluence of two rivers and suspension bridge

cities.name: Bumthang
cities.notes: Spiritual heartland with Jakar Dzong, ancient temples and yak cheese

itinerary.sim: B-Mobile tourist SIM — easiest pickup at Paro Airport or Bhutan Telecom shops in Thimphu

Thimphu.name: Thimphu
Thimphu.note: Use Thimphu as the acclimatization stop after landing in Paro and keep one full day for culture-heavy sightseeing.

activities.name: Scenic transfer from Paro Airport to Thimphu via the Wang Chhu valley
activities.tip: Most operators include this drive; keep the window seat for the first river-valley views.

activities.name: National Memorial Chorten
activities.tip: A small donation of about ₹50 is appreciated if you linger inside the prayer circuit.

activities.name: Tashichho Dzong evening exterior and courtyard visit
activities.tip: Late afternoon is best once government offices close and the light softens over the dzong.

activities.name: Clock Tower Square and handicraft market stroll
activities.tip: Weekend evenings are the liveliest for people-watching and casual shopping.

days.theme: Buddha Views, Museums & Bhutanese Everyday Life

activities.name: Buddha Dordenma
activities.tip: Go in the morning before haze builds up across the valley.

activities.name: Folk Heritage Museum
activities.tip: Pair it with lunch nearby for a good introduction to farmhouse life.

activities.name: Motithang Takin Preserve
activities.tip: Bhutan’s national animal is easiest to spot in the cooler hours.

activities.name: Changlimithang Archery Ground
activities.tip: Stand well back and watch for the singing-and-dancing celebrations after each hit.

activities.name: National Textile Museum
activities.tip: Worth adding if you want context before shopping for handwoven pieces.

Punakha.name: Punakha
Punakha.note: Punakha works best after Thimphu because the descent via Dochula Pass naturally breaks the journey and keeps the route efficient.

activities.name: Dochula Pass and the 108 Druk Wangyal Chortens
activities.tip: Aim to cross before 10 AM for the clearest Himalayan views.

activities.name: Druk Wangyal Lhakhang
activities.tip: The murals here are unusually modern and worth the short stop.

activities.name: Punakha Dzong
activities.tip: Spring jacaranda bloom makes this one of Bhutan’s prettiest dzong visits.

activities.name: Punakha Suspension Bridge
activities.tip: Visit close to sunset when the valley light is softer and winds are calmer.

days.theme: Valley Hikes, Fertility Temple & Village Life

activities.name: Chimi Lhakhang
activities.tip: The temple is reached by an easy paddy-field walk, so carry sun protection rather than hiking gear.

activities.name: Khamsum Yulley Namgyal Chorten hike
activities.tip: Go early or late to avoid the hottest valley hours on the exposed trail.

activities.name: Talo village cultural walk
activities.tip: Ask your guide to include a farmhouse tea stop if you want a local interaction.

activities.name: Mo Chhu white-water rafting
activities.tip: This is a good optional swap for travelers wanting a more active second day.

Paro.name: Paro
Paro.note: Keep two nights here so Tiger’s Nest gets a full dedicated hiking day instead of being rushed around arrival or departure.

days.theme: Ancient Temples, Dzong Views & Paro Town

activities.name: Kyichu Lhakhang
activities.tip: Go around dusk if you want a quieter, more atmospheric temple visit.

activities.name: Drugyel Dzong viewpoint
activities.tip: Clear mornings give you the best chance of seeing Chomolhari behind the ruins.

activities.name: Paro town heritage walk and handicraft browsing
activities.tip: Bhutan Post’s stamp counter is a nice stop for compact souvenirs.

activities.name: Rinpung Dzong riverside approach and cantilever bridge
activities.tip: The bridge and dzong together are most photogenic in late afternoon.

activities.name: Paro Taktsang (Tiger’s Nest) hike
activities.tip: Start by 7–8 AM, carry at least 1.5L of water, and expect a full 5–6 hour outing.

activities.name: Horse ride to the midway cafe (optional)
activities.tip: Horses only go to the halfway point, not all the way to the monastery.

activities.name: Tiger’s Nest cafeteria tea stop
activities.tip: This is the classic viewpoint for a rest before the final staircase section.

activities.name: National Museum of Bhutan (Ta Dzong)
activities.tip: A strong backup if clouds or fatigue shorten your post-hike plans.

Bumthang.name: Bumthang
Bumthang.note: Bumthang is the spiritual extension for travelers who want quieter valleys and older temple history beyond western Bhutan.

activities.name: Drukair flight from Paro to Bumthang
activities.tip: Seats are limited, so ask your operator to secure this sector 4–6 weeks ahead.

activities.name: Jakar Dzong
activities.tip: This is an easy first stop after landing because it offers a fast overview of the valley.

activities.name: Kurje Lhakhang
activities.tip: Dress modestly and take time inside the oldest temple linked to Guru Padmasambhava.

activities.name: Swiss Guest House farm stop for local cheese and Red Panda beer
activities.tip: Good place to sample buckwheat dishes if you want something region-specific.

activities.name: Jambay Lhakhang
activities.tip: Go early if you want the oldest temple stop before tour groups arrive.

activities.name: Tamshing Lhakhang
activities.tip: This is the best stop for travelers who enjoy mural-heavy temple interiors.

activities.name: Membartsho (Burning Lake)
activities.tip: Treat this as a sacred site rather than a picnic stop; the gorge is narrow and quiet.

activities.name: Ura Valley excursion
activities.tip: Add this only if you are staying a second night and want a slower village-focused finish.

connections.method: Private transfer via the Wang Chhu valley (about 1.5 hrs)
Private transfer via Dochula Pass (about 2–2.5 hrs)
Private transfer via Thimphu bypass (about 3.5 hrs)
Drukair domestic flight to Bathpalathang Airport (about 35 min)
Drukair domestic flight or full-day road return via Trongsa

extras: Indian travelers do not need a visa, but you must carry a valid passport or voter ID and travel through a registered Bhutanese operator for permits.
Budget around ₹8,400 per person per day for the Sustainable Development Fee; this is the biggest fixed cost in Bhutan.
Spring (March–May) and autumn (September–November) are the easiest seasons for Tiger’s Nest, Dochula views, and clearer domestic flight operations.
Dress modestly for dzongs and temples: covered shoulders, covered knees, and no photography inside shrine rooms unless explicitly allowed.
Bhutan is largely cash-light in hotels but smaller cafes and roadside stalls may still prefer cash, so keep some INR or BTN handy.
